mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which forces equity markets to price in lower corporate revenues and yields negative forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.03 during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which signals immediate economic momentum and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3.5% forces central banks to adopt restrictive monetary policy, which increases borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60d returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical relationship with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth signals broad-based economic expansion and higher corporate profit margins, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 acceleration predicts negative returns during mid-cycle expansions, or the signal out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.05 in quarterly forward return regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with falling unemployment indicates a synchronized supply-demand recovery that boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 5-day returns when INDPRO rises and UNRATE falls, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.
